title: Bureau of Legislative Affairs
text:
Our Mission
The Bureau of Legislative Affairs (H) coordinates all legislative activity for the Department of State, proudly serves as the Department’s main interlocutor to Congress, and advises the Secretary, the Deputies, as well as the Under Secretaries and Assistant Secretaries on legislative strategy. In 2025, H supported 3,441 separate congressional engagements, including 406 between the Department of State and Members of Congress. H coordinated 487 Congressional trips overseas, 36 hearings, 2349 correspondence letters, 613 Congressional report submissions, and confirmation of 78 nominees. The Capitol Hill Liaison Offices, maintained by the Bureau inside the Capitol, handled over 15,538 policy and consular casework inquiries while also facilitating programs to sustain close collaboration with Congress. Consular Affairs handled an additional 160,000 inquiries from Congress in 2025.
